funFacts.text: The Statue of Liberty was a gift from France in 1886 and weighs about 225 tons.
The US is the only country to have landed humans on the Moon — 12 astronauts walked on its surface.
Americans eat approximately 3 billion pizzas every year, roughly 23 pounds per person.
Alaska is so large that it could fit Texas inside it more than two times.
The US invented jazz, blues, rock and roll, hip-hop, and country music.

culture.music: Birthplace of jazz, blues, rock and roll, hip-hop, and country music.
culture.art: Home to the Metropolitan Museum of Art and pioneered pop art through Andy Warhol.

geography.climate: Extremely diverse — from arctic in Alaska to tropical in Hawaii and Florida.
geography.terrain: Vast central plains, western mountains (Rockies, Sierra Nevada), eastern forests (Appalachians).

timeline.title: Declaration of Independence
timeline.description: The thirteen colonies declared independence from Great Britain.

timeline.title: End of Civil War
timeline.description: The Union prevailed, slavery was abolished via the 13th Amendment.

timeline.title: Moon Landing
timeline.description: Apollo 11 astronauts became the first humans to walk on the Moon.

funFacts.text: The British drink about 165 million cups of tea per day.
The British monarchy is the oldest in Europe, dating back over 1,200 years.
There are over 1,500 castles scattered across the United Kingdom.
William Shakespeare invented over 1,700 words still used in English today.
The UK has produced 137 Nobel Prize winners — third most in the world.

culture.music: Gave the world The Beatles, The Rolling Stones, Adele, and the British Invasion of the 1960s.
culture.art: Home to the Tate Modern and National Gallery; birthplace of Turner and Banksy.

geography.climate: Temperate maritime with mild winters and cool summers; famously rainy.
geography.terrain: Rolling hills, Scottish Highlands, Lake District, chalk cliffs of Dover.

timeline.title: Norman Conquest
timeline.description: William the Conqueror defeated King Harold at the Battle of Hastings.

timeline.title: Magna Carta
timeline.description: King John signed the Magna Carta, limiting royal power.

timeline.title: End of World War II
timeline.description: Britain celebrated victory after six years of war.

funFacts.text: The Eiffel Tower was supposed to be temporary and was almost torn down in 1909.
France produces over 1,600 varieties of cheese — more than any other country.
France is the world's most visited country, welcoming over 89 million tourists annually.
The croissant actually originated in Austria, not France.
The Louvre is the world's most visited museum with 9.6 million visitors per year.

culture.music: Famous for chanson, Edith Piaf, and a thriving electronic music scene (Daft Punk).
culture.art: Capital of Impressionism; home to Monet, Renoir, and the Louvre Museum.

geography.climate: Temperate in the north, Mediterranean in the south, alpine in the east.
geography.terrain: Flat plains in the north, Alps and Pyrenees mountains, Mediterranean coast.

timeline.title: French Revolution
timeline.description: The storming of the Bastille marked the beginning of the end for absolute monarchy.

timeline.title: Napoleon Crowned Emperor
timeline.description: Napoleon Bonaparte crowned himself Emperor of the French at Notre-Dame.

timeline.title: Liberation of Paris
timeline.description: Paris was freed from Nazi occupation during World War II.

funFacts.text: Germany has over 1,500 different beers brewed according to the 1516 Reinheitsgebot purity law.
The Autobahn has sections with no speed limit — some drivers exceed 250 km/h.
Germany is the origin of the Christmas tree tradition, dating back to the 16th century.
The first printed book (Gutenberg Bible) was produced in Germany around 1455.
Neuschwanstein Castle inspired Disney's Sleeping Beauty Castle.

culture.music: Home of Bach, Beethoven, and the world-renowned Berlin Philharmonic.
culture.art: Birthplace of Bauhaus design movement; Expressionism through artists like Kirchner.

geography.climate: Temperate with warm summers and cold winters; wetter in the west.
geography.terrain: Northern plains, central uplands, Bavarian Alps in the south, Rhine Valley.

timeline.title: German Unification
timeline.description: The German Empire was proclaimed at Versailles under Otto von Bismarck.

timeline.title: End of World War II
timeline.description: Nazi Germany surrendered unconditionally, ending the war in Europe.

timeline.title: German Reunification
timeline.description: East and West Germany reunified after the fall of the Berlin Wall.

funFacts.text: Mount Fuji last erupted in 1707, and over 300,000 people climb it every year.
Japan's bullet trains (Shinkansen) have an average delay of less than 1 minute per year.
There are more Michelin-starred restaurants in Tokyo than in any other city in the world.
Japan has more robots per capita than any other country, with over 300,000 industrial robots.
Japan has over 6,800 islands, though only about 430 are inhabited.

culture.music: Traditional gagaku court music alongside J-Pop; invented karaoke.
culture.art: Ukiyo-e woodblock prints inspired Impressionism; anime is a global cultural export.

geography.climate: Ranges from subarctic in Hokkaido to subtropical in Okinawa; monsoon season in summer.
geography.terrain: Mountainous volcanic archipelago; 73% of the land is mountains and forests.

timeline.title: Meiji Restoration
timeline.description: Imperial rule was restored, launching Japan's rapid modernization.

timeline.title: End of World War II
timeline.description: Japan surrendered after atomic bombings, beginning a new era of peace.

timeline.title: Tokyo Olympics
timeline.description: The first Olympics held in Asia showcased Japan's postwar recovery.

funFacts.text: The Amazon Rainforest produces about 20% of the world's oxygen.
Brazil has won the FIFA World Cup 5 times — more than any other country.
Rio Carnival is the world's largest carnival, attracting 2 million people per day.
Brazil is the world's largest coffee producer, supplying about 1/3 of global coffee.
Brazil has the highest biodiversity of any country, with over 50,000 plant species.

culture.music: Birthplace of samba, bossa nova, and forró; heavily influenced global music.
culture.art: Oscar Niemeyer's modernist architecture; vibrant street art scene in São Paulo.

geography.climate: Mostly tropical; equatorial in the north, temperate in the south.
geography.terrain: Amazon basin, Brazilian Highlands, Pantanal wetlands, Atlantic coast.

timeline.title: Portuguese Arrival
timeline.description: Pedro Álvares Cabral landed in Brazil, claiming it for Portugal.

timeline.title: Independence
timeline.description: Prince Pedro declared Brazil's independence from Portugal.

timeline.title: Rio Olympics
timeline.description: Brazil hosted the first Olympic Games in South America.

funFacts.text: The Taj Mahal took 22 years and 20,000 workers to build, completed in 1653.
India invented the number zero, the decimal system, and the game of chess.
Bollywood produces over 1,800 films per year — more than any other film industry.
India is the world's largest producer and consumer of spices.
Indian Railways employs over 1.3 million people, one of the largest employers on Earth.

culture.music: Classical traditions of Hindustani and Carnatic music; Bollywood film scores.
culture.art: Ancient cave paintings at Ajanta; intricate Mughal miniatures; contemporary art boom.

geography.climate: Tropical monsoon in the south, temperate in the north, arid in the west.
geography.terrain: Himalayas in the north, Thar Desert, Deccan Plateau, coastal plains.

timeline.title: First War of Independence
timeline.description: The Sepoy Mutiny marked the first large-scale revolt against British rule.

timeline.title: Independence
timeline.description: India gained independence from British rule under Mahatma Gandhi's leadership.

timeline.title: Mars Orbiter Mission
timeline.description: India became the first Asian nation to reach Mars orbit on its first attempt.

funFacts.text: Australia has more kangaroos than people — about 50 million kangaroos vs 26 million humans.
The Great Barrier Reef is the largest living structure on Earth, visible from space.
Australia is home to 21 of the world's 25 most venomous snakes.
Australia has over 10,000 beaches — you could visit a new one every day for 27 years.
The platypus is one of only five species of egg-laying mammals in the world.

culture.music: Birthplace of AC/DC, INXS, and Kylie Minogue; didgeridoo is the world's oldest wind instrument.
culture.art: Aboriginal dot paintings represent the oldest continuous art tradition (65,000+ years).

geography.climate: Mostly arid interior; tropical in the north, temperate in the southeast.
geography.terrain: Vast outback deserts, Great Dividing Range, tropical rainforests, coastal beaches.

timeline.title: Captain Cook's Arrival
timeline.description: James Cook claimed the east coast of Australia for Britain.

timeline.title: Federation
timeline.description: The six colonies united to form the Commonwealth of Australia.

timeline.title: Sydney Olympics
timeline.description: Australia hosted a celebrated Olympic Games in Sydney.

funFacts.text: Italy has 58 UNESCO World Heritage Sites — more than any other country.
There are over 600 different pasta shapes registered in Italy.
Visitors throw an estimated €1.5 million into Rome's Trevi Fountain each year.
The piano was invented in Italy by Bartolomeo Cristofori around 1700.
Italy is home to Ferrari, Lamborghini, Maserati, and Alfa Romeo.

culture.music: Birthplace of opera; Verdi, Puccini, and Vivaldi are among the greatest composers.
culture.art: The Renaissance began here — Leonardo da Vinci, Michelangelo, and Raphael.

geography.climate: Mediterranean in the south, continental in the north, alpine in the mountains.
geography.terrain: Alpine north, Po Valley, Apennine mountain spine, coastal Mediterranean.

timeline.title: Founding of Rome
timeline.description: According to legend, Romulus founded Rome on the Palatine Hill.

timeline.title: Italian Unification
timeline.description: The Kingdom of Italy was proclaimed under King Victor Emmanuel II.

timeline.title: Italian Republic
timeline.description: Italians voted to abolish the monarchy and establish a republic.

funFacts.text: The Great Wall of China is over 21,000 km long and took over 2,000 years to build.
China is the only country where giant pandas live in the wild — about 1,800 remain.
China uses about 80 billion pairs of disposable chopsticks per year.
Gunpowder, paper, printing, and the compass were all invented in China.
China has the world's largest high-speed rail network — over 42,000 km.

culture.music: Traditional instruments like erhu and guzheng; rich operatic traditions (Peking Opera).
culture.art: Renowned for calligraphy, silk painting, porcelain (china), and jade carving.

geography.climate: Varies from tropical in the south to subarctic in the northeast.
geography.terrain: Himalayan plateau in the west, deserts in the northwest, fertile plains in the east.

timeline.title: First Emperor
timeline.description: Qin Shi Huang unified China and began building the Great Wall.

timeline.title: People's Republic Founded
timeline.description: Mao Zedong proclaimed the People's Republic of China.

timeline.title: Beijing Olympics
timeline.description: China hosted the Olympic Games, showcasing its modernization.

funFacts.text: The ancient Aztecs used cacao beans as currency and invented chocolate drinks.
Mexico has 35 UNESCO World Heritage Sites, more than any other country in the Americas.
Every winter, 300 million monarch butterflies migrate to Mexico from Canada.
Mexico is the origin of corn (maize), tomatoes, vanilla, and avocados.
Día de los Muertos (Day of the Dead) is a UNESCO-recognized tradition dating back 3,000 years.

culture.music: Mariachi music is UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage; also known for norteño and cumbia.
culture.art: Frida Kahlo and Diego Rivera revolutionized Mexican and global art.

geography.climate: Desert in the north, tropical in the south, temperate in the highlands.
geography.terrain: Sierra Madre mountain ranges, Yucatán Peninsula, Sonoran Desert, volcanic belt.

timeline.title: Tenochtitlan Founded
timeline.description: The Aztecs founded their capital on an island in Lake Texcoco.

timeline.title: War of Independence
timeline.description: Miguel Hidalgo's cry of Dolores sparked the independence movement.

timeline.title: Mexican Revolution
timeline.description: A decade-long revolution reshaped Mexico's political and social landscape.

funFacts.text: The Great Pyramid of Giza is the only surviving Wonder of the Ancient World.
Ancient Egyptians worshipped cats and shaved their eyebrows to mourn a cat's death.
Egyptians invented papyrus paper, toothpaste, and one of the first written languages.
The Suez Canal handles about 12% of all global trade — over 19,000 ships per year.
The Great Sphinx is about 4,500 years old and its nose remains a mystery to historians.

culture.music: Umm Kulthum is the most famous Arab singer of all time; oud is the iconic instrument.
culture.art: Ancient pharaonic art; intricate Islamic geometric patterns and calligraphy.

geography.climate: Hot desert climate; very little rainfall; scorching summers.
geography.terrain: Sahara Desert covers 96% of Egypt; fertile Nile Delta and Valley.

timeline.title: Unification of Egypt
timeline.description: King Menes unified Upper and Lower Egypt, founding the first dynasty.

timeline.title: Suez Canal Opens
timeline.description: The Suez Canal opened, connecting the Mediterranean and Red Sea.

timeline.title: Egyptian Revolution
timeline.description: Mass protests led to the end of President Mubarak's 30-year rule.

funFacts.text: South Korea is the birthplace of esports and has the world's fastest average internet speed.
The K-beauty industry is worth over $13 billion; Koreans pioneered the 10-step skincare routine.
BTS became the first K-pop act to top the Billboard Hot 100 chart.
Koreans consume about 40 pounds of kimchi per person per year.
Seoul's Incheon Airport has been rated the world's best airport multiple times.

culture.music: K-Pop is a global phenomenon; traditional gugak music dates back centuries.
culture.art: Celadon pottery is world-renowned; Hangul script is considered a design masterpiece.

geography.climate: Continental with hot, humid summers and cold, dry winters; monsoon in summer.
geography.terrain: Mountainous east, flat western coastal plains, volcanic Jeju Island.

timeline.title: Liberation from Japan
timeline.description: Korea was freed from 35 years of Japanese colonial rule.

timeline.title: Seoul Olympics
timeline.description: South Korea showcased its modernization to the world.

timeline.title: Parasite Wins Oscar
timeline.description: First non-English film to win Best Picture at the Academy Awards.

funFacts.text: South Africa has 11 official languages — more than almost any other country.
The world's largest diamond, the Cullinan (3,106 carats), was found in South Africa in 1905.
African penguins live on the beaches of Cape Town — one of the few places penguins live in Africa.
The Springboks' 1995 Rugby World Cup win united the nation after apartheid.
The world's first heart transplant was performed in Cape Town in 1967.

culture.music: Township jazz, Afrikaans folk, and Amapiano have shaped global music.
culture.art: Rich tradition of Zulu beadwork, Ndebele murals, and contemporary art in Johannesburg.

geography.climate: Mostly semiarid; subtropical on the east coast, Mediterranean around Cape Town.
geography.terrain: Interior plateau (highveld), Drakensberg mountains, coastal lowlands.

timeline.title: Dutch Settlement
timeline.description: Jan van Riebeeck established the first European settlement at the Cape.

timeline.title: Apartheid Begins
timeline.description: The National Party instituted apartheid, a system of racial segregation.

timeline.title: End of Apartheid
timeline.description: Nelson Mandela was elected president in the first democratic election.

funFacts.text: The Spanish siesta tradition dates back thousands of years — shops still close at 2 PM.
La Tomatina festival sees 150,000 tomatoes thrown in just one hour.
The Running of the Bulls in Pamplona has been held annually since 1591.
Spanish is the world's second most spoken language by native speakers (over 470 million).
Spain's La Liga is one of the most watched sports leagues on Earth.

culture.music: Flamenco is a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage; vibrant pop and reggaeton scene.
culture.art: Home of Picasso, Dalí, and Gaudí's surreal architecture in Barcelona.

geography.climate: Mediterranean on the coast, continental on the plateau, oceanic in the north.
geography.terrain: Central plateau (Meseta), Pyrenees, Sierra Nevada, coastal plains.

timeline.title: Columbus Discovers America
timeline.description: Christopher Columbus sailed from Spain, reaching the Americas.

timeline.title: Spanish Armada
timeline.description: Spain's Invincible Armada was defeated by England, ending Spanish naval dominance.

timeline.title: New Constitution
timeline.description: Spain transitioned to democracy after Franco's dictatorship.

funFacts.text: Russia spans 11 time zones — the most of any country in the world.
Russia launched the first satellite (Sputnik, 1957) and first human (Yuri Gagarin, 1961) into space.
Siberian forests (taiga) produce more oxygen than the Amazon Rainforest.
The Hermitage Museum in St. Petersburg has over 3 million items — it would take 11 years to see each one for 1 minute.
The coldest inhabited place on Earth is Oymyakon, Russia, where temperatures hit −67.7°C.

culture.music: Tchaikovsky, Rachmaninoff, and Stravinsky shaped classical music worldwide.
culture.art: Russian avant-garde (Malevich, Kandinsky); Fabergé eggs are national treasures.

geography.climate: Arctic in the north, subarctic in Siberia, continental in the center, subtropical on the Black Sea coast.
geography.terrain: Vast Siberian plains, Ural Mountains, Caucasus Mountains, tundra, taiga forests.

timeline.title: Russian Revolution
timeline.description: The Bolshevik Revolution ended the Russian Empire and established the Soviet Union.

timeline.title: First Human in Space
timeline.description: Yuri Gagarin became the first person to orbit Earth aboard Vostok 1.

timeline.title: Dissolution of USSR
timeline.description: The Soviet Union dissolved into 15 independent republics.

funFacts.text: Canada produces 71% of the world's maple syrup — 13 million gallons per year.
Hockey is the official winter sport, and Canada has won more Olympic hockey golds than any nation.
Canada has the longest coastline in the world at 243,042 km.
Two-thirds of the world's polar bears live in Canada.
Canada is officially bilingual — English and French are both national languages.

culture.music: Home to Drake, Celine Dion, Joni Mitchell, and The Weeknd.
culture.art: Group of Seven painters captured Canada's wilderness; Inuit soapstone carvings.

geography.climate: Arctic in the north, subarctic, continental, and temperate on the coasts.
geography.terrain: Rocky Mountains, Canadian Shield, prairies, boreal forests, Arctic tundra.

timeline.title: Confederation
timeline.description: The British North America Act united four provinces into the Dominion of Canada.

timeline.title: Maple Leaf Flag
timeline.description: Canada adopted its iconic red and white maple leaf flag.

timeline.title: Vancouver Olympics
timeline.description: Canada topped the gold medal count at the Winter Olympics for the first time.

funFacts.text: Istanbul is the only city in the world that spans two continents — Europe and Asia.
Tulips originated in Turkey, not the Netherlands — they were exported to Holland in the 1500s.
Turkish coffee is UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage and has been brewed since the 15th century.
Turkey has more ancient Greek ruins than Greece itself.
Cappadocia's fairy chimneys were formed by volcanic eruptions millions of years ago.

culture.music: Ottoman classical music, Turkish folk, and arabesque pop; home of whirling dervishes.
culture.art: İznik tiles, Ottoman calligraphy, and miniature painting traditions.

geography.climate: Mediterranean on the coast, continental in the interior, oceanic on the Black Sea.
geography.terrain: Anatolian plateau, Taurus and Pontic Mountains, coastal lowlands, volcanic peaks.

timeline.title: Constantinople Founded
timeline.description: Roman Emperor Constantine made Byzantium (Istanbul) the new capital.

timeline.title: Fall of Constantinople
timeline.description: The Ottoman Empire conquered Constantinople, ending the Byzantine Empire.

timeline.title: Republic of Turkey
timeline.description: Mustafa Kemal Atatürk founded the Republic of Turkey.

funFacts.text: Georgia is the birthplace of wine — archaeologists found 8,000-year-old wine residue in clay vessels.
Georgia has 5 UNESCO World Heritage Sites, including ancient cave monasteries.
The Georgian alphabet is one of only 14 unique scripts still in use in the world.
Georgian polyphonic singing is a UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage masterpiece.
Ushguli, one of Europe's highest continuously inhabited settlements, sits at 2,200 meters.

culture.music: UNESCO-recognized polyphonic singing dates back centuries; toastmaster (tamada) traditions.
culture.art: Medieval frescoes, goldsmith work, cloisonné enamel, and the Gelati Monastery murals.

geography.climate: Subtropical on the Black Sea coast, continental in the east, alpine in the mountains.
geography.terrain: Greater Caucasus Mountains, lush Colchic lowlands, semi-arid eastern plains.

timeline.title: United Georgian Kingdom
timeline.description: King Bagrat III unified the Georgian kingdoms, beginning a golden age.

timeline.title: First Republic
timeline.description: Georgia declared independence, establishing the Democratic Republic of Georgia.

timeline.title: Independence Restored
timeline.description: Georgia declared independence from the Soviet Union.

funFacts.text: Nollywood produces over 2,500 films per year — second only to India's Bollywood.
Nigeria is Africa's most populous country with over 220 million people and 250+ ethnic groups.
Nigeria is home to the endangered Cross River gorilla — fewer than 300 remain in the wild.
Afrobeats, pioneered by Fela Kuti, has become one of the world's most popular music genres.
Wole Soyinka became the first African to win the Nobel Prize in Literature in 1986.

culture.music: Fela Kuti created Afrobeats; Burna Boy, Wizkid, and Davido are global superstars.
culture.art: Benin Bronzes are among the finest metal sculptures ever made; Nok terracottas date to 500 BC.

geography.climate: Tropical in the south, semiarid in the north; rainy season from April to October.
geography.terrain: Coastal swamps, tropical forests, savanna grasslands, Jos Plateau.

timeline.title: Independence
timeline.description: Nigeria gained independence from the United Kingdom.

timeline.title: Biafran War
timeline.description: A civil war erupted when southeastern Nigeria declared independence as Biafra.

timeline.title: Return to Democracy
timeline.description: Nigeria transitioned from military to civilian democratic rule.
